compiled_state.title: Write regression test for sub-agent CLI browser automation without MCP
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description
Write a regression test that validates a sub-agent can perform browser automation using @playwright/cli without MCP tool access. This directly satisfies done definition 2.

Test approach: Create a test script that simulates the sub-agent dispatch scenario — verifies the playwright-debug skill instructions would direct an agent to use CLI commands (navigate + screenshot) without any MCP tool dependency.

The test creates a mock skill context (no browser_* tools available), then verifies:
1. The skill instructions reference @playwright/cli commands for navigation and screenshots
2. The pre-flight check would detect @playwright/cli availability
3. Session cleanup is included in the instructions

This is a behavioral test that exercises the skill file as a consumed instruction set, not a grep-for-strings test.

## File Impact
- tests/skills/test-playwright-debug-cli-regression.sh (CREATE)
- .test-index (EDIT — add mapping)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Regression test file exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x tests/skills/test-playwright-debug-cli-regression.sh
- [ ] Test contains at least 3 test functions
  Verify: grep -c "^test_" tests/skills/test-playwright-debug-cli-regression.sh | awk '{exit ($1 < 3)}'
- [ ] All tests pass
  Verify: bash tests/skills/test-playwright-debug-cli-regression.sh 2>&1 | grep -q "passed"
- [ ] .test-index entry maps regression test
  Verify: grep -q "test-playwright-debug-cli-regression" .test-index
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh passes (exit 0)
  Verify: bash tests/run-all.sh
